Transcrição Completa: Day 3 - Email Outreach Follow-up Strategy

 

[00:00] Day number three. Hope you're doing well. You've sent 200 emails out. Hopefully, if you have not hit your numbers, what do you do? Do you catch up? Do you stay behind? That's completely up to you.

 

[00:08] Hopefully, you understand that you can compound three times. So, maybe the numbers that you're not hitting now, you can make up for then. But guess what? That's still not an excuse to go anywhere below your 100 per day.

 

[00:20] You have to hit that number. It's a discipline. Now, here's the thing. You might be thinking in your head, you've sent 200 emails up to this point. If you're actually following and disciplining yourself to do so, you will have people that will start communicating back with you.

 

[00:35] Jeremy, what do I say afterwards? Right? What's the process itself? Soon as you reach out on a cold email, the logical next step is they're either going to communicate back and be like, dude, what the fuck is all this data that you just sent me? This is a lot of stuff. Like, what does all this mean?

 

[00:52] They might also say something like, "Wow, this is awesome. I'm glad that you sent all this." Like, like what what do you want to do? Like, what do you what is it you do? Like, why are you sending this to me?

 

[01:03] I've gotten people that straight up will tell you things like, "Dude, get the fuck out of here. Why are you sending me this stuff? I don't care about you. Like, why are you pitching me?"

 

[01:11] Uh, you'll have other people that'll say things like, "Hey, is there a time we can chat?" They'll be really ambiguous, broad, just crazy.

 

[01:18] Here's what you want to know. Okay? It doesn't matter what anybody says. Here's what you want to say after what anybody says back to you. And if they don't say anything back to you, doesn't matter what they say once again back to you, you need to take the stance to follow up.

 

[01:33] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Average Close Statistics"

 

NÚMEROS: 5th-12th follow-up attempt (old data), 8-17 follow-up attempts (current estimate), 193 follow-ups for $15K deal, 7 follow-ups for $100K monthly deal

 

VISUAL: Representação de estatísticas de fechamento, comparação entre tentativas antigas vs atuais, exemplos de casos extremos e casos de sucesso rápido

 

[01:35] The average close won't happen until the fifth to 12th follow-up attempt. I think those numbers are higher now. I actually think it's between about eight and sometimes 17.

 

[01:44] I had to follow up with a guy 193 times in order to close him for 15k. That's a whale. But that's not even shit. I've closed 100k monthly deals that I've only had to follow up with seven times.

 

[01:58] Your communication cycles are going up because there's more of you. So you have to be more impressive than the other people that are going to be talking to these people, right?

 

[02:07] So once again, you've got 200 communications. Somebody communicates back. You've done your followup to this point to get them to communicate back. You're watching them in the HubSpot email tracker. Open emails.

 

[02:19] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Data-Driven Email Strategy"

 

NÚMEROS: 200 emails enviados (Day 3 milestone)

 

VISUAL: Fluxo de trabalho mostrando envio de email → rastreamento HubSpot → resposta → follow-up, representação visual de email com dados vs abordagem tradicional de vendas

 

[02:21] What do you say if somebody's open and they don't respond? You're going to tell them oneliners because you've hopefully either sent them a video or a long ass email with a bunch of data in it.

 

[02:31] Either one of those a little overwhelming to a business owner. Think of their life real quick. They're in the middle of their day doing a bunch of shit that has nothing to do with you. You come out of nowhere out of left field literally asking them for money.

 

[02:44] Technically, that's why I like the data driven approach more than anything else. You're showing them themselves. You're literally just saying, "Here, I want to show you a bunch of stuff about you."

 

[02:54] That gains interest psychologically into them wanting to talk to you, which is the whole point of the data driven email or with the use Loom video once again that you can take of a screen capture of the same exact data for a little speedier process in real time.

 

[03:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Follow-up One-Liner Scripts"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Lista de scripts de follow-up formatados como mensagens curtas e diretas, ênfase em linguagem não-agressiva e centrada no cliente

 

[03:10] If they do not communicate back with you, your follow-up lines can be something like this:

 

[03:15] "Hey, know you're busy. Wanted to follow up. Make sure you saw this. Simple."

 

[03:20] "Hey, sure you're slammed. I get it. Hopefully, you're doing great in business. I just wanted to help out a little more. Hope you saw this email. Let me know if you let me know if you got a chance to look through it."

 

[03:32] "Hey, sure this data was a little overwhelming. I would love an opportunity to kind of explain it a little bit. Not trying to hard sell or anything. Just wanted to make sure I gave you guys value as I saw a bunch of opportunities."

 

[03:45] You know, simple little oneliner snippets that aren't about you getting money. It's completely about the fact you're acknowledging that they're a busy business owner and you're in the middle of interrupting their day while they're doing a bunch of shit like building their business.

 

[04:00] You do not have the opportunity to be selfish here and project your emotions onto the situation. Go hit your hundred. Quit being soft and in real time understand that after they communicate back with you, it's really simple.

 

[04:13] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Next Step Strategy - Phone Call"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Fluxo de progressão: Email → Resposta → Agendamento de Zoom/Call, representação de transição de comunicação assíncrona para síncrona

 

[04:15] No matter what they say, the next logical conversation is, "Let's talk on the phone, baby. Come on, let's get in a screen share on a computer. Can I send you a Zoom link? I'd love to go through this data."

 

[04:25] That's the whole point of the data driven email is the followup that you get after they communicate. You get an opportunity to have a conversation about something instead of being in an awkward situation where I have to tell you some random scripts to say.

 

[04:38] You know what you're going to be able to talk about? You're going to be able to talk about all the shit that you found on them because you went deep versus wide, you sent them a data driven email.

 

[04:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Deep vs Wide Strategy Benefits"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Comparação visual entre abordagem "deep" (profunda - poucos clientes, muitos dados) vs "wide" (ampla - muitos clientes, poucos dados), benefícios documentados em cada lado

 

[04:50] That's also, oh my god, this all of a sudden makes sense for your notes on the fact that every time you communicate with this client, oh my god, you have every bit of data that you could ever possibly need because you sent a data driven email went deep instead of wide.

 

[05:05] Which benefits them when you send them an email, which benefits you because you get documented information that you yourself get to reference back on.

 

[05:13] And I don't know if you know how email works in an email thread that you can also go and read the conversation with that individual person.

 

[05:20] So by the time that they say whatever they say, your communication back is going to be, hey, love to talk about all this stuff. I'm sure it can be a little overwhelming. So I completely get it. You're busy.

 

[05:32] All these things, not trying to hard sell you straight up. Just want to make sure that you understand what they can do for your business. Thought they'd get you some additional results. They're a good time of day in the morning, afternoon that we can chat.

 

[05:44] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Power of Choice Psychology"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Comparação de opções de resposta: "Yes/No" vs "Morning/Afternoon" ou "10 AM/2 PM", setas mostrando como escolhas direcionadas levam a respostas desejadas

 

[05:46] Always want to give options. Don't just say one or the other. If you give somebody a response, it's yes or no. What are the options in the answer? You see that? Yes or no? What are the answers?

 

[05:57] If I give somebody morning or night or morning or afternoon or 10 or two, is it yes or no? No, it's morning or afternoon. You see what you can do with the power of choice?

 

[06:07] Give people the right choices, they'll make the right answers. You give them the right choices, they'll give you the right answers that you're looking for.

 

[06:15] If you're not selfish and you're not trying to in real time talk about you and talk about the money that you want and instead you use your data driven email for their benefit for your benefit a little down the road once people start communicating back.

 

[06:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Advanced Follow-up Script Template"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Script completo formatado como template de email, destacando frases-chave sobre qualificação, valor e posicionamento premium

 

[06:30] And on top of that, guess what? That's what your follow-up is. Hey, would love to chat about what you saw above. I'm looking in real time for some new clients that we'd like to add in to our client bracket.

 

[06:42] Listen, we're taking on a few heavy hitters, taking on a few trophies we want to put up on our shelf. We're looking to see who's qualified.

 

[06:49] We took a look at your business, gave you some data that we knew would help you and that we'd love to deliver value on. Absolutely. If we can figure something out, we'd love to have a conversation about how we can work together.

 

[07:01] But listen, that time's not going to be forced upon you. I'm truly here to give you an unbiased perspective that you might not see every day on your business.

 

[07:09] I'm not another one of these guys calling you and just trying to pitch you on my services. Absolutely. I sell services. Absolutely. I have an agency. Absolutely. We crush it for you.

 

[07:18] Like I said, if you'd like to be a trophy on our shelf, that is my intention is to build a relationship with you because this is business and I do want to benefit you getting more leads, results, sales, attention.

 

[07:30] I just want to make sure that you're better off in this particular area that I pointed out. What time of day works for you? Morning, afternoon.

 

[07:38] Go hit your hundred.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

200 emails enviados até o Dia 3

Meta diária obrigatória: 100 emails

Estatística de fechamento antiga: 5-12 tentativas de follow-up

Estatística de fechamento atual: 8-17 tentativas de follow-up

Caso extremo: 193 follow-ups para fechar deal de $15K

Caso de sucesso rápido: 7 follow-ups para fechar deal de $100K mensal

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Abordagem Data-Driven: Envio de emails com dados detalhados sobre o negócio do prospect

Deep vs Wide: Focar em poucos prospects com pesquisa profunda vs muitos prospects com abordagem superficial

Rastreamento HubSpot: Monitorar abertura de emails para timing de follow-up

Opção Loom Video: Screen capture dos dados para processo mais rápido

Psicologia da Escolha: Oferecer opções específicas (manhã/tarde) ao invés de sim/não

Posicionamento Premium: "Trophy clients" e "heavy hitters" para criar exclusividade

Follow-up One-Liners: Scripts curtos que reconhecem a ocupação do prospect

 

ESTRUTURA:

Disciplina diária de 100 emails não-negociável

Sistema de 3x compounding para recuperar números

Processo: Email → Rastreamento → Resposta → Follow-up → Agendamento de Call

Transição de comunicação assíncrona (email) para síncrona (Zoom/telefone)

Benefício duplo: dados para o prospect + documentação para o vendedor

Thread de email como CRM natural para referência futura

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Deal de $15K fechado após 193 follow-ups

Deal de $100K mensal fechado após 7 follow-ups

Ênfase em não mencionar dinheiro nos follow-ups iniciais

Foco em valor e resultados ao invés de preço

 

MENTALIDADE E ABORDAGEM:

Não ser egoísta ou projetar emoções na situação

Reconhecer que você está interrompindo o dia do business owner

Posicionar-se como consultor imparcial, não vendedor agressivo

"Quit being soft" - manter disciplina nos números

Transparência sobre ser agência que vende serviços

Construir relacionamento genuíno focado em beneficiar o cliente

 

DURAÇÃO: 7:38 FORMATO: Vídeo educacional direto ao ponto / Training de vendas
